svg.text: 6×6 Grid Layout · Per-Pico GPIO Assignments · Page 2 of 2
MOTOR GRID — FRONT VIEW (facing the wall)
Large # = Motor ID · P0 = Pico 0 · GP3 = GPIO pin 3 · byte N = SPI frame position
PICO 0 — PICO_ID=0 — SPI bytes 0–8 — Top-Left Quadrant
PICO 1 — PICO_ID=1 — SPI bytes 9–17 — Bottom-Left Quadrant
PICO 2 — PICO_ID=2 — SPI bytes 18–26 — Top-Right Quadrant
PICO 3 — PICO_ID=3 — SPI bytes 27–35 — Bottom-Right Quadrant
1. Find motor number on the grid (left)
2. Read Pico ID (P0–P3) and GPIO pin (GP0–GP8) from the cell
3. Trace the wire from that Pico's GPIO pin to the motor/ESC
Each Pico drives 9 motors via PWM on GPIO 0–8. Motors are wired in row-major order within their 3×3 quadrant:
GPIO 0, 1, 2 → First row of quadrant · GPIO 3, 4, 5 → Second row · GPIO 6, 7, 8 → Third row
Motor signal wire connects: Pico GPIO pin → ESC signal input. ESC powers the motor independently.
PWM range: 1000–2000 μs (byte value 0–255 maps linearly). Min running speed: 1200 μs.
Active Wind Wall — ISM WiMi · Motor Mapping · Page 2 of 2 · Print at A4 Landscape
